methodsAttempted.notes: Bulgarian-language search found official faculty strategy pages mentioning AI and generative AI, plus AI degree-program pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uni-sofia.bg изкуствен интелект Софийски университет правила политика етика
methodsAttempted.notes: Search found the official Philologia journal publication ethics page and official research/news/program pages.
The Faculty of Philosophy strategy page was verified by Firecrawl and HTTP HEAD; robots.txt disallows only /wp-admin/.
The Philologia journal ethics page is JavaScript-rendered; Firecrawl extracted the official Bulgarian body text.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery did not locate a central Sofia University generative-AI policy for all students or staff. The staged claims are therefore narrowly scoped to an official Faculty of Philosophy professional-area strategy and an official Sofia University-published journal ethics page. Official degree-program and news/event pages were rejected as non-policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official sources support directly scoped claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide rules from faculty, journal, news, research, or program pages.
Do not stage raw HTML, rendered pages, PDFs, screenshots, or browser logs.

artifacts.claimText: For Sofia University's Faculty of Philosophy professional area 3.5 strategy, planned activities include updating specialized software for research and teaching, including generative AI, and providing opportunities for staff training in AI use.

artifacts.claimText: The Sofia University-published Philologia journal's publication ethics page says the journal will not consider manuscripts where AI use is traced, and it lists AI use, including compilation and paraphrase of two or more works, within its plagiarism examples.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Осигуряване на възможности за преподавателите да развиват и надграждат своите знания и квалификация... обучение за ползване на изкуствен интелект. Обновяване на компютърната и офис техника, както и специализиран софтуер за научните изследвания и учебната дейност, включително генеративен изкуствен интелект.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow faculty/professional-area strategy claim; not a university-wide AI policy or binding student rule.
Approved only for the Philologia journal publication-ethics context; it must not be generalized to all Sofia University coursework or research outputs.

artifacts.title: Sofia University St. Kliment Ohridski AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Sofia University is limited to narrow official-source claims: one Faculty of Philosophy professional-area strategy referencing AI training and generative-AI software for research and teaching, and one Sofia University-published Philologia journal ethics page addressing AI use in manuscripts. No central university-wide generative-AI policy was verified during this crawl.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The verified sources are not a central university-wide AI policy and should be presented with their faculty, professional-area, or journal scope.
Official research, news, event, and degree-program pages mentioning AI were rejected where they lacked policy-specific guidance.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
